phrasalVerbs.definition: To stop people from entering an area by putting something such as a rope around it.
examples: Police cordoned off the crime scene immediately.
The street was cordoned off for the filming.
They cordoned off the damaged section of the building.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To move towards someone quietly and slowly, especially because you want to surprise them.
examples: The cat crept up on the mouse silently.
Don't creep up on me like that—you scared me!
Old age has a way of creeping up on you.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To prevent someone from going somewhere; to prevent something from taking place.
examples: Security guards headed off the protestors before they reached the building.
We need to head off any potential problems early.
She headed off the argument by changing the subject.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To stop someone or something from moving forwards; to prevent progress.
examples: The police held back the crowd during the protest.
Don't let fear hold you back from pursuing your dreams.
Barriers held back the flood waters for several hours.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To leave secretly or without being noticed.
examples: She slipped away from the party without saying goodbye.
The opportunity slipped away before we could act.
He slipped away while everyone was distracted.

phrasalVerbs.definition: If people in a group spread out, they move away from one another so that they cover a large area.
examples: The search party spread out across the forest.
Let's spread out and look for clues separately.
The volunteers spread out to cover more ground.

fillInExercises.sentence: The authorities ___ the area after the accident.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cordon off' means to restrict access to an area.

fillInExercises.sentence: Deadlines have a way of ___ up on you unexpectedly.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Creep up on' means to approach quietly or happen gradually.
'Head off' means to prevent something from happening.

fillInExercises.sentence: Security guards ___ back the enthusiastic fans at the concert.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Hold back' means to prevent someone from advancing.

fillInExercises.sentence: She ___ from the boring meeting without anyone noticing.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Slip away' means to leave quietly and secretly.

fillInExercises.sentence: The rescue team ___ out to search the entire valley.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Spread out' means to disperse over a larger area.

fillInExercises.sentence: Don't let self-doubt ___ you from achieving your goals.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Hold back' means to prevent or restrain.

fillInExercises.sentence: Police ___ off the entire street during the investigation.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cordon off' means to block access to an area.

lines.speaker: Alex
lines.text: The police have [cordoned off] the entire block.

lines.speaker: Fatima
lines.text: They need to [head off] any curious onlookers.

lines.speaker: Alex
lines.text: The officers have [spread out] to search for evidence.

lines.speaker: Fatima
lines.text: Let's hope the suspect didn't [slip away] already.

lines.speaker: Alex
lines.text: They're trying to [hold back] the media too.

lines.speaker: Fatima
lines.text: We should leave before someone [creeps up on] us here.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Make sure Tom doesn't [slip away] before we're ready.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: I'll [head off] any suspicions he might have.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Everyone should [spread out] around the room.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: We can [creep up on] him when he enters.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Try to [hold back] your excitement until he arrives.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Should we [cordon off] the back entrance just in case?
